title: Feed Discovery, URL Identity, Scope, and SSRF Boundary · Execution Trace: Feed Go Discovery Provenance
summary: Users can submit arbitrary URLs, discovery follows every alternate link, redirects change authority silently, and hostname checks occur only before DNS resolution. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go discovery provenance increment in authorized feed discovery and network-scope policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Discovery Provenance · Theory Feed Go Discovery Provenance
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 7501 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile bounded feed discovery, URL identity, SSRF decisions, and provenance; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
